choice: Manual entry is per-Holding and matches the storage model: for a marketable security (kind a) the user enters a SHARE QUANTITY (we value it at the latest price), for an asset-class proxy (b) or non-market fixed/cash (c) they enter a DOLLAR BALANCE; every manual entry carries an as-of date defaulting to today. Do NOT allow a free-floating 'account total' with no holdings — an Account's value is always the sum of its Holdings' valuations. Allow a quick 'cash' Holding (kind c) as the escape hatch for a balance the user doesn't want to break out.
rationale: 9b7cc94d already fixes that marketable holdings are quantity×price and proxies/cash are dollar balances; manual entry must produce exactly those shapes or it can't feed daily valuation or reconcile with a later E*TRADE sync of the same Account. An account-level total with no holdings would be un-valuable daily and un-mappable to a synced position, breaking precedence (777f97e5). The cash-Holding escape hatch keeps it low-friction for accounts the user tracks only as a lump sum.
